id: rfc.0001.concern
type: rfc

domain: domain
summary: Proposes one consequential change for review before implementation.
depends-on: none
---
# RFC 0001: title

## Problem

Describe the current limitation with evidence.

## Dependencies

- Hard: None.
- Conditional: None.
- Integration: None.

List only direct relationships.
Every hard dependency must also appear by stable document id in `depends-on` and link to its RFC here.
Conditional dependencies block only a named part of this proposal; integration dependencies require alignment only when both proposals are implemented.
Use `None.` exactly for an empty category, and treat every RFC link on these three entries as a dependency edge that must appear in the RFC index.

## Goals

List outcomes this proposal must achieve.

## Non-goals

Bound the proposal explicitly.

## Proposed design

Describe behavior, architecture, formats, and ownership at the level needed for review.

## Alternatives

Compare credible options and tradeoffs.

## Compatibility and migration

Describe data, behavior, platform, and rollout effects.

## Validation

Define tests, observability, and acceptance criteria.

## Open questions

List decisions that still block acceptance.

## Promotion plan

List the architecture, specification, reference, decision, and user/development documents this RFC will create or update if accepted.
After implementation or rejection, migrate every retained fact and inbound link, then delete the RFC.
